title: Shopping & Markets
description: Ask prices, bargain politely, and buy what you need at a palengke or store.
situation: Buying things: asking prices, sizes, and haggling gently at the local market.

phrases.tip: Point and say ito (this) or iyan (that one near you).
Naman softens it, making your complaint sound friendly.
Haggling is expected at a palengke (wet market), not in malls.
Offer a lower number to start the friendly back-and-forth.

phrases.native: May iba pa po bang kulay?
phrases.pronunciation: may ee-BA pa po bang KOO-lay

phrases.english: Do you have a bigger size?

phrases.tip: Say this as you hand over your money or fare.
Pahingi (please give me) is a handy everyday request word.
A warm way to close a transaction and promise to return.
